person.name: Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard

nameForms.fullText: Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir John de Weston of Weston and Newton, son of Sir Hugh de Weston and a dau. of Audley of Newport, b c1287 and d. bef. 5 May 1349.

He was married twice; m.(1) Isabella de Bromley, father of his heir Thomas and five daughters, on the death of Robert his grandson, daughters Anna d. unm and Elizabeth (de Peshall) succeeded their brother as co-heiresses. Sir John m.(2) Isolda de Newton, and had a son Robert de Weston, the ancestor of the Westons of Rugeley and became the main stem of the family after the death of Thomas. Sir John de Weston held the important post of Constable of Bordeaux under Edward III and died c. 1330.

«b»Children by Isolda de Newton:«/b»
1.) Robert, ancestor of the Westons of Rugeley and Lichfield.

2.) William

3.) Alice

4.) Agnes

1277: Born per 15 Oct 1305 inquisition into death of his father which confirmed John son of Hugh de Weston, aged 28, as next heir.

7 Sep 1299: Held land in Blymhill.

1306: Knighted with the Prince of Wales (Edward II) and summoned to attend the Prince with horses and arms against the Scots.

1308: Sued the Abbot of Buildwas for £16.

1309: Patron of church of Weston.

18 Mar 1309: Exchange of various rights to lands in Neuton with Ralph de Hampton.

1313: Granted a messuage to John Bron of Weston at a rent of 8s. 9d.

9 Aug 1314: Summoned to war in Scotland ub retinue of William de Montague.

1315: Plea against John de Weston for land in Neuton held by William son of John de Neuton.

18 Sep 1317: Will of Isabella, wife of John de Weston.

2 October 1317: Probate granted.

1321: Agreement that John de Saundersted and wife Elizabeth to hold manor of Robaston for life, with reversion to John, son of John de Weston.

29 Sep 1322: Leased land in Weston to Richard Carpenter for 5s. p.a. for 20 years.

1327: One twentieth of moveable goods assessed at 5s. in Weston Hewes and 3s. in Blymhill in support of war in Scotland.

1328: Gave land in Blumenhall to his son John.

1330: Gave land in Weston to Adam le Porter and wife Sara.

18 Feb 1331: Gave lands in Neuton to his sons Robert and John.

2 Jun 1331: Declares no further diminishment of patrimony for son Thomas.

8 Jun 1335: Received letters of protection in suite of John, Earl of Cornwall to the Scottish wars.

18 Aug 1339: Conceded land in Blumenhall by Roger de Pichford.

3 Oct 1340: Gave messuage in Weston to son Robert, remainder to siblings William and Agnes.

26 Jan 1346: Gave property in Weston to daughter Isolda, remainder to her sister Elena.

16 Oct 1347: All demands on John de Weston superseded for a man at arms, a hobelar and an archer.

5 May 1349: Dead, since son Thomas assumes the style of lord of Weston

sibling.name: Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard

spouse.name: Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Weston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Weston, from Old English west ‘west’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

topographic name from Middle English atte west tun(e) ‘at the west farmstead, settlement’ (Old English west + tūn), or from Middle English (bi) weste(n)tune (Old English be westan tūne or be west in tūne), denoting someone who lived either to the west of a settlement or in the western part of a settlement. The latter was a common surname in Sussex.

variants.name: Wesson
meanings: English (Midlands and Kent): variant of   Weston  .
History: John Wesson came from England to Salem, MA, in 1644.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard was born in April 1276, in Weston Coyney, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hugh de Weston, was 52 and his mother, Agnes de Longespée, was 50. He had at least 1 son with Margery Romeyn. He died in 1349, in Stafford St Chad, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard(이)가 1276년 4월 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 웨스턴 코이니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de Weston(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Longespée(은)는 50세였다. 그는 Margery Romeyn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1349년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드 스트 채드에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.
